title: Project Title
description: A short, specific sentence explaining what the project is, who it serves, and the value it creates.
metaDescription: A detailed project case study by Fiza Mansoor.

hero.placeholderHint: Replace this area with a descriptive project image
hero.alt: Placeholder for the main project image
hero.caption: A concise caption that explains what the reader is seeing.

blocks.title: Overview
blocks.intro: Start with the context a new reader needs. Describe the project, its purpose, and why it mattered in two or three clear sentences.
blocks.guide: the main project goals, your contribution, your discipline, and a clear definition of success.

blocks.title: Goals
items: State the most important project goal.
Describe what success needed to look like.
Include a practical or user-focused objective.

blocks.title: My contribution
blocks.content: Explain exactly what you owned, what you collaborated on, and how your discipline contributed to the project.

blocks.alt: Placeholder for a project overview image
blocks.caption: Use this caption to introduce what the project image shows.

blocks.title: Process
blocks.intro: Share the moments that changed the work, rather than trying to document every task.
blocks.guide: the evidence you gathered, alternatives you explored, decisions you made, feedback you received, and how the work improved.

blocks.title: Research
blocks.content: Explain what you needed to understand first and which findings shaped the direction of the project.
blocks.show: research notes, precedents, site analysis, user needs, requirements, or source data.

image.caption: Add research notes, source data, site analysis, or another image that supports your findings.

blocks.title: Design
blocks.content: Present the early ideas you explored and explain why one direction became more promising than the others.
blocks.show: sketches, options, diagrams, concepts, moodboards, or design principles.

image.alt: Placeholder for an early design image
image.caption: Use a sketch, concept, option study, or diagram that shows how the design direction emerged.

blocks.title: Development
blocks.content: Describe how the selected idea became more resolved, practical, coordinated, and technically credible.
blocks.show: drawings, models, prototypes, calculations, specifications, or coordination work.

image.caption: Add a developed drawing, model, prototype, calculation, or coordinated technical detail.

blocks.title: Testing
blocks.content: Share the checks, feedback, simulations, reviews, or observations used to evaluate the developing work.
blocks.show: test results, review comments, comparisons, validation criteria, or user feedback.

image.caption: Show test results, feedback, a comparison, simulation output, or evidence used to assess the work.

blocks.title: Refinement
blocks.content: Explain what changed after testing and how those final decisions improved clarity, usability, or performance.
blocks.show: before-and-after comparisons, final adjustments, resolved details, or lessons applied.

image.alt: Placeholder for the refined project design
image.caption: Use a final detail or before-and-after comparison to make the refinement visible.

blocks.title: The challenge
blocks.intro: Write the central problem clearly from the user, client, or project point of view.
blocks.guide: evidence that the problem exists, who it affects, why it matters, and the constraints or competing priorities that made it difficult.

blocks.title: Problem #1 — Problem name
content: Explain the main gap, risk, inefficiency, unmet need, or opportunity in clear and specific terms.
Evidence: Add the observation, data, feedback, or condition that proves this problem exists.

blocks.title: Problem #2 — Problem name
content: Explain the second issue and how it compounds, influences, or conflicts with the first problem.
Consequence: State what happens if this problem is not addressed.

blocks.title: Key insight
blocks.content: Highlight the research finding or realization that changed the direction of the work.

blocks.alt: Placeholder for evidence of the project challenge
blocks.caption: Show the condition, data, comparison, or constraint that makes the challenge understandable.

blocks.title: Outcome and impact
blocks.intro: Describe the final result and connect it back to the original goals. Use measured outcomes where they are available and be clear when results are qualitative.

notes.text: Show the thinking, not only the final image.

content: Final outcome: Explain what you produced and how the completed work addressed the original challenge.
Impact: Describe what improved for users, the client, the environment, project performance, or future work.
Evidence of success: Include any relevant measurements, testing, feedback, approval, comparison, or observed result. Mention limitations honestly when evidence is qualitative.

blocks.placeholderHint: Use the strongest image of the finished work
blocks.alt: Placeholder for the final project image
blocks.caption: Describe the final work and its most important feature.

blocks.title: Reflection
blocks.intro: Close the case study with an honest assessment of the work and what it changed in your practice.
blocks.guide: what worked, what you learned, what you would change, and the most valuable next step.

blocks.title: What worked well
blocks.content: Identify a decision, method, collaboration, or result that made the project stronger.

blocks.title: What I learned
blocks.content: Share a specific lesson about the work, collaboration, technical process, or your own practice.

blocks.title: What I would do next
blocks.content: Describe the next test, improvement, or phase you would prioritise with more time.

blocks.title: Final takeaway
blocks.content: End with one concise sentence that captures why this project matters to you and what it demonstrates about your approach.
